prompt: Which task is the BEST fit for AI rather than deterministic automation?
choices: Applying a fixed discount from a known rate table
Validating that a phone number has a valid format
Summarizing the themes across 300 free-text survey answers
Sorting a list of orders by date
explanation: Summarizing themes across unstructured free text is fuzzy, open-ended language work where AI excels. The others are exact, known rules that deterministic code does more cheaply, reliably, and repeatably.

prompt: Select ALL good reasons to prefer deterministic automation when the rule is exact and stable. (Select all that apply.)
choices: It gives the same answer every time for the same input
It is typically cheaper and faster per run
It is easier to test and audit
It is non-deterministic, which is desirable for exact rules
explanation: Deterministic code is repeatable, cheap, fast, testable, and auditable — ideal for exact, known rules. The last option is false: exact rules want determinism, and AI is the non-deterministic one.

prompt: A weekly KPI report is pulled from the database and emailed on a fixed schedule. What is the best approach?
choices: An AI agent that decides each week what to include
A deterministic scheduled job that runs the query and sends the email
An LLM that writes the SQL fresh every week
Fine-tune a model on past reports
explanation: The steps are fixed and known, so it is plain automation. Wrapping fixed steps in an 'AI agent' is agent-washing — it adds cost, latency, and non-determinism for no benefit.

prompt: Which of these is the WRONG use of an LLM?
choices: Drafting a first-pass email reply for a human to edit
Summarizing long meeting notes
Computing the exact total of an order
Classifying messy support tickets by intent
explanation: Computing an exact total is deterministic arithmetic — a formula does it exactly, instantly, and for free. Asking a model to do it is slower, costs money, and can be confidently wrong. The others are fuzzy language or judgment tasks that fit AI.

prompt: A feature must draft a customer reply AND show the customer's exact balance on the same screen. What is the best design?
choices: Use AI for both, to keep one consistent technology
Use deterministic code for both
AI drafts the reply; a database query returns the exact balance (hybrid)
Buy an end-to-end SaaS suite so a vendor handles everything
explanation: The reply is fuzzy language (AI); the exact balance is a known lookup (deterministic). Using each tool where it fits — a hybrid — is the right design. Never ask a model to produce an exact figure.
